channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: The Trump administration plans to end U.S. funding for Gavi, a global program that purchases shots to help vaccinate children in developing countries against some of the world’s deadliest diseases.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Sudan’s army chief returns to Khartoum for first time in 2 years of war after airport recaptured
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's army said Wednesday it had recaptured Khartoum's international airport, and the military chief flew back to the capital for the first time in nearly two years of war, bringing the military closer to regaining full control of the city from the rival Rapid Support Forces paramilit
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 American soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have died, NATO leader says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, but that he did not yet know the details. A U.S. official would say only that the four soldiers were involved in a training accident.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 24 dead as wildfires ravage southern South Korea and force thousands to evacuate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Wind-driven wildfires that were among South Korea’s worst ever have ravaged the country’s southern regions, killing 24 people, destroying more than 300 structures and forcing 28,800 residents to evacuate, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices has unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German authorities raid group that allegedly aims to overthrow Eritrean government
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Police were executing raids in six German states Wednesday against a group that authorities say “is part of an international network whose aim is to overthrow the government in Eritrea.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia has convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia focuses foreign aid spending on Pacific region as US slashes aid programs
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia plans to spend a larger share of its foreign aid on its neighbors in Asia and the Pacific islands after the United States announced major cuts to development and humanitarian aid abroad.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai prime minister survives a challenge by rivals who say her father is pulling the strings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra easily survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday, following a two-day debate in which rivals charged that she has mismanaged the country and let her father, a former prime minister, control her administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds in Gaza take to streets to protest Hamas
item.description: Hundreds of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip took to the streets Tuesday for a rare protest against the Palestinian militant group Hamas, which has controlled the war-torn strip for nearly two decades.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: America's allies alarmed by a leaked group chat about attack plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As wake-up calls go, the alarms don’t get much louder. Allies of the United States see the group chat between top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, EMMA BURROWS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Motorcyclist who vanished into sinkhole is found dead following search
item.description: A motorcyclist was found dead Tuesday around 18 hours after he vanished into a massive sinkhole that opened up in Seoul, South Korea’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Russia, Ukraine agree to ‘eliminate the use of force’ in Black Sea
item.description: The White House announced Tuesday it came to an agreement with Ukraine and Russia to “eliminate the use of force” in the Black Sea, as well as end strikes on energy facilities, marking one of the more significant steps the Trump administration has seen toward a stop in fighting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, UN envoy says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, the top U.N. official in the world’s youngest nation warned on Monday, lamenting the government’s sudden postponement of the latest peace effort.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio says South Africa must protect White farmers, US will offer admission to those facing violence
item.description: White South Africans and other minorities facing violence in that country could possibly be welcome for admission to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows 'highly skilled' pilot's final moments before fatal air show crash
item.description: South African pilot James O’Connell killed over the weekend in crash during air show at the Saldanha Bay Airfield, in an incident captured on video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military leaders discuss Ukraine peacekeeping force as partial ceasefire plans are worked out
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Senior military officers from more than 30 countries across Europe and beyond met in England on Thursday to flesh out plans for an international peacekeeping force for Ukraine as details of a partial ceasefire are worked out. U.K.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taliban release an American man who was abducted while traveling in Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American man who was abducted more than two years ago while traveling through Afghanistan as a tourist has been released by the Taliban in a deal with the Trump administration that Qatari negotiators helped broker, the State Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9
